OUTLAWS.Definition: people who brοke the law and were wanted by the authorities

SLAUGHTERED.Definition: killed in a very cruel and violent way

BORDERLANDS.Definition: areas near the border between the two places

NATIONHOOD.Definition: the state of being an independent country or people

NOMADIC.Definition: moving from place to place instead of living permanently in one area

COUNTER RAIDS.Definition: attacks made in response to earlier attacks

RETREATING.Definition: moving back from danger or fighting

CYCLE.Definition: a series of events that kept happening again and again

MASSACRE.Definition: the killing of many people at the same time

QUEST FOR REVENGE.Definition: a long effort to get revenge

FREEDOM FIGHTER.Definition: a person who fought against control by another group

TERROR.Definition: a person or thing causing great fear

THE NAME STUCK.Definition: the name continued to be used

THIRST FOR REVENGE.Definition: a very strong desire for revenge

RESERVATION.Definition: an area where Native Americans were required to live

DEFINED AREA.Definition: an area with clear limits or borders

ASSIMILATION.Definition: the process of making people adopt another culture and way of life

FOLD.Definition: include as part of a larger group

WASTELAND.Definition: land that was poor and difficult to use

UNDERSUPPLIED.Definition: not given enough food, equipment, or other things needed

SCOUTS.Definition: people sent ahead to gather information

BLUNT.Definition: direct and not trying to be polite

UNSUSTAINABLE.Definition: unable to continue for a long time

BARELY FARMABLE.Definition: only just good enough for growing crops

HUMILIATION.Definition: a feeling of great embarrassment or shame

OVERWHELMING FORCE.Definition: a very large and powerful amount of military strength

INTERMEDIARY.Definition: a person who helped the two sides communicate

PRISONER OF WAR.Definition: a person captured by an enemy during a war

UNDER THE TERMS.Definition: according to the agreed rules or conditions

PRACTICAL CALCULATION.Definition: a decision based on what was useful or sensible

MARTYR.Definition: a person who dies for a cause or belief

EXILE.Definition: being forced to live away from their home

HUMID.Definition: warm and wet in the air

SWAMPY.Definition: covered with very wet ground and water

DWELLINGS.Definition: homes or places where they lived

PRISON WITHOUT THE BARS.Definition: a place where people could not leave even though there were no prison bars

ADAPT.Definition: change in order to fit the new situation

CONVERTED.Definition: changed to (a different religion or belief)

MYTH-MAKING MACHINE.Definition: a system that turned real people into legends and stories

MEMENTO.Definition: an object kept to remember him

CAPITALIST.Definition: a person who tried to make money through business

INAUGURAL.Definition: happening at the beginning of an important event or period

CEREMONIAL DRESS.Definition: special clothing worn for an important event

AUTOBIOGRAPHY.Definition: a book about his life written by himself

DEDICATED.Definition: wrote it in honour of him

PNEUMONIA.Definition: a serious illness affecting the lungs

SET IN.Definition: started and begun to have an effect

DEATHBED.Definition: the bed on which he died

RIDING INTO THE SUNSET.Definition: leaving in a happy or heroic way at the end of the story

SAVAGE.Definition: a very violent and cruel man

WARRIOR.Definition: a person who fought in battles or wars
